compliance_cache_used: sprint/compliance-cache/idaho.json (FIRST Idaho page — wrote state_income_tax, sui, withholding_forms, deposit_cadence, minimum_wage, local_payroll_tax blocks for siblings to reuse)

editorial_v2_applied.capsule_pattern: 4-paragraph stake → size → ack → promise (no 'Answer.cite this' label)
editorial_v2_applied.named_scenarios: Logan (Boise dev), Sage (Coeur d'Alene sales lead), Wyatt (Idaho Falls ops manager) used across Q-sections
editorial_v2_applied.h2_mix: Statements ('The SUI wage base jumped to $58,300 for 2026', 'Idaho follows the federal $7.25 minimum wage', 'How Teamed runs Idaho payroll end to end') + direct questions ('What is the Idaho state income tax rate in 2026?', 'What is Form ID W-4 and why does Idaho require it?', 'How does an Idaho employer file withholding?', 'Does Idaho have a local payroll tax?')
editorial_v2_applied.statutes_in_tables_only: All Idaho Code §, 26 U.S.C. §, 29 U.S.C. § references confined to Source columns in tables; body prose has zero statute references
editorial_v2_applied.no_em_dashes_in_body: Em-dashes confined to inline visual labels and pricing/Zero FX hyphens; body prose uses commas/periods/parentheses
editorial_v2_applied.disclaimer_present: Page-disclaimer aside above meta-footer, with Idaho State Tax Commission + Idaho Department of Labor references
editorial_v2_applied.inline_visual: pad-note in ID W-4 section ('Two W-4s on day one, not one.') — NOT stamp-block per v2 guidance

high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty:prompt_correction — prompt cited 'HB 521 switched Idaho to FLAT income tax 5.3% in 2024'; verified actual is HB 40 (2025) signed 6 March 2025 by Governor Brad Little, retroactive to 1 January 2025, locked 5.3%. HB 521 (2024) was the EARLIER cut to 5.695%. Page uses HB 40 / 5.3% / 2025 retroactive — and includes the full rate-history table showing both bills.

factual_uncertainty:prompt_correction — prompt cited 'UI wage base $55,300ish 2026'; verified actual 2026 wage base is $58,300 per Idaho Department of Labor 2026 release (up from $55,300 in 2025). Page uses $58,300.
factual_uncertainty:resolved — prompt cited new-employer UI rate '~1.0%'; verified actual is exactly 1.0% (the minimum allowed under federal conformity 26 U.S.C. § 3303(b)(1)). Page uses 1.0%.
factual_uncertainty:resolved — Form ID W-4 confirmed mandatory per Idaho State Tax Commission Employer Withholding Guide and update-W-4 page (separate from federal W-4).

factual_uncertainty:low — Experience-rated band figures (positive 0.208%–0.694%, negative 1.25%–5.4%) cited from Idaho Department of Labor 2026 rate schedule as reported by Idaho Payroll Guide and Bloomberg Tax; IDOL primary site sometimes returns 403 to scrapers, so direct verification via the Employer Portal recommended before any client-specific rate quote.

factual_uncertainty:low — Idaho HB 485 (2025) minimum-wage proposal status confirmed not-enacted as of May 2026; bill could reactivate in 2026 legislative cycle; monitor Idaho Legislature active bills quarterly.
link_gate_warn:Idaho neighbour and parent pages rendered as plain text per link gate (targets ship wave 3-4)
